result:
Filled 28 decisions to `/workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/patron-anima-eval/agent-output/spider-page-for-oculus.yaml`.

**Distribution:**
- 11 high / 17 medium / 0 low
- 0 custom answers, 0 abstentions

**Shape of the answers:**
- **Soft dependency & typing (D1–D3):** Recommend Oculus, import its types, type handlers via hono — hold hard lines on the contract (#15) even when it costs a little coupling.
- **Extend existing APIs (D6, D8, D17, D18, D27):** Consistently prefer fabricating uses of what exists (#27, #3, #36) over new custom routes or duplicative guild-scanning logic — `listDesigns()` / `listBlockTypes()` belong on the apparatus APIs.
- **Thin-but-complete MVP (D10, D11, D19, D20, D23):** Strong bias toward vanilla tech, minimum columns, status+writId filters, tabular views — earn new structure from a second consumer (#18, #23).
- **Content over metadata (D9, D13, D15, D20):** Content-bearing engine summaries and readonly `<pre><code>` over counts and `<textarea>` affordances (#41, #24).
- **Precedent-setting (D4, D21, D27):** `id: spider` / title: `Rigging`, `/api/spider/<resource>` noun paths, reused badge classes — all conscious of #13 (first live writer sets the convention).

**Closest calls:** D3 (hono dep leakage), D5 (static asset convention), D22 (scan-vs-trust), D26 (auto-refresh vs MVP simplicity). These were the medium-confidence picks where principles partially conflicted; none reached the threshold for `custom`.
